printer_verbatim: The 'Civil and Military Gazette' Press, Lahore.
title: The Urdu Punjab Record, Part I, Volume XLV, No. 6, Judicial, for June, 1910
price: Annual subscription, Rs. 8, annas 4; single copy, annas 12
copyright: The Proprietors, the 'Civil and Military Gazette' Press, Lahore; No. 164, dated the 25th October, 1910.
notes: Completes the CMG Punjab Record run for the quarter (8 issues registered, all copyrighted).

title: Anath Hiteshi Anath Alya Arya Samaj Ferozepore ka Mahwari Risala
gloss: A Monthly Magazine of the Arya Samaj Orphanage at Ferozepore, dealing with income and expenditure. Volume VI, No. 6, for August, 1910. Edited by Lala Jagan Nath, Ferozepore.
notes: Orphanage accounts published monthly — Arya Samaj institutional transparency print (fundraising by audit); orphanages were the shuddhi movement's demographic front line.

title: Anath Hiteshi, Volume VI, No. 7, for September, 1910
gloss: Edited by Lala Jagan Nath, Ferozepore.

title: Monthly Circular of the Punjab Hindu Family Mutual Relief Fund, Lahore, Volume XV, No. 5, for the month of August, 1910
publisher: The Secretary of the fund named
notes: The Urdu edition of the fund circular: 6,000 copies vs Hindi 950 and Punjabi 1,050 (p37, consecutive regs 1239–1241) — the script-market hierarchy quantified again this quarter; note the Urdu series is at Volume XV while Hindi/Punjabi are at Volume VIII (the vernacular-script editions began seven years later).

title: Sadhu
gloss: The 'Sadhu Magazine,' Volume VII, No. 6, for December, 1910, a special number containing a translation in Urdu verse of Shrimad Bhagwad Gita by Lala Suraj Narayan, Mehr. Edited by Babu Shiva Brat Lal, M.A., Lahore.
price: Annual subscription, Rs. 3, anna 1; single copy, 6 annas
copyright: L. Suraj Narayan Mehr, Reporter on Books, Education Department, Punjab, Lahore; No. 181, dated the 21st November, 1910.
notes: The Reporter on Books' SECOND authorial appearance this quarter (cf. Kalam-i-Mehr, p28 s70): his Urdu verse Bhagavad Gita fills the Sadhu Magazine special number, he holds copyright No. 181, and he signs this catalog at the foot of this same page (Lahore, 13th February 1911) — cataloguer, poet, and copyright proprietor in one office. penciled 'Spec.' in pencil. Editor Shiva Brat Lal is the noted Radhasoami figure.
marks: X; pencil 'Spec.' left margin; faint pencil right margin
